name: Indianapolis Bankruptcy Law Office of Eric C. Lewis
description_short: Indianapolis-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings. Lewis Legal Services helps individuals eliminate debt and achieve fresh financial starts through experienced legal counsel.
description_long:
Lewis Legal Services, P.C., operated by attorney Eric C. Lewis, is a general practice law firm based in Indianapolis, Indiana with a primary focus on consumer bankruptcy law. The firm has established itself as a local resource for individuals facing overwhelming debt and seeking legal relief under federal bankruptcy protections. Mr. Lewis is described as an experienced bankruptcy attorney who has counseled hundreds of people and families through the bankruptcy process.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy services covering both Chapter 7 liquidation bankruptcy and Chapter 13 reorganization bankruptcy. Chapter 7 services help clients discharge and eliminate most debts while retaining exempt property for a fresh financial start. Chapter 13 services focus on debt reorganization and restructuring, often allowing clients to pay back only a fraction of owed amounts through a court-approved repayment plan. The firm also provides ancillary services including estate planning, probate administration, notary public services, and general legal practice. Online virtual consultations are now available, and the firm offers flexible scheduling with early morning, evening, and Saturday appointments by special request.

The firm emphasizes personalized client attention and affordability as distinguishing factors. Their marketing materials highlight compassion, professionalism, and tailored case management as core values. The firm positions itself around the concept of a "fresh start" and debt elimination, with messaging focused on stress reduction and financial freedom. They have established resources including a Chapter 13 client payment portal, bankruptcy FAQs, helpful educational videos, and forms and resources pages.

As a federal debt relief agency properly registered to help clients file under the U.S. bankruptcy code, Lewis Legal Services operates within the established legal framework. The firm's scope is limited to legal representation in bankruptcy matters and related estate planning services—they are not a debt settlement or credit repair company. Their location at 1060 East 86th Street serves the Indianapolis metropolitan area.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Experienced bankruptcy attorney with track record of counseling hundreds of clients
Offers both Chapter 7 (liquidation) and Chapter 13 (reorganization) bankruptcy options
Provides online virtual consultations for accessibility and convenience
Flexible appointment scheduling including early morning, evening, and Saturday options
Emphasizes affordability and personalized attention to each client's case
Operates as registered federal debt relief agency compliant with bankruptcy code
Provides supplementary services including estate planning, probate, and notary services
cons: Limited to Indianapolis area with single physical location; no evidence of remote representation for out-of-state clients
Website lacks specific attorney credentials, years of experience, or detailed case results/testimonials
No transparent pricing information provided on website—consultation required for cost estimates
General practice firm with bankruptcy focus, not a bankruptcy-exclusive specialist shop
Contact primarily through phone/email with note that consultations are 'by appointment only,' potentially limiting quick access
best_for: Indianapolis-area individuals considering Chapter 7 bankruptcy seeking debt discharge and fresh start
Chapter 13 debtors needing debt restructuring and reorganization with manageable repayment plans
Individuals preferring personalized local legal counsel over online-only bankruptcy filing services
People also needing estate planning or probate services alongside bankruptcy relief
diagnosis: This firm is best for Indianapolis-area individuals and families facing serious financial distress who qualify for and benefit from formal bankruptcy protection under Chapter 7 or Chapter 13. The main caveat is that while the firm offers professional bankruptcy legal representation, the website lacks detailed information on attorney qualifications, case outcomes, or pricing—and services appear limited to the Indianapolis region, making it unsuitable for consumers outside central Indiana seeking representation.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
